rationale: Both candidates correctly identify the same two real fixes (DiffusionOutput indexing/attribute access and the already_submitted guard removal) and both ground the orchestrator.py:1290 reasoning in the same stage_pool.py submit_initial/submit_update list-rejection logic the ground-truth inline comment relies on. X presents this cleanly with precise file:line citations and a concise, actionable non-blocking test suggestion. Y covers similar ground but buries it in redundant raw tool-report JSON, duplicates the same findings twice, and contains an internally contradictory assumption ('could not inspect due to tool budget exhaustion' vs. claiming it verified submit_initial), which hurts trustworthiness and makes the actual actionable asks harder to extract.
